Comments: Not on ECHA but the single publication that used this chemical reported the manufacturer so I was able to use the manufacturer’s SDS
Chemical name is Pentapotassium bis(peroxymonosulphate) bis(sulphate)
SDS appeared comprehensive so I did not break down into components

SDS was unclear (no acute aquatic H statement but report says ‘Very toxic’ in acute section) so I considered each of the two ingredients separately using section 3 of the SDS by considering worst case

SDS was unclear (it was not considered harmful to aquatic life but there were no tests on the combined compound so separate results should have carried through) so I used ECHA data for each of the two ingredients separately considering worst case

Could not find SDS but chemical is used in only one publication which gives 4 active ingredients: ethacridine lactate, acriflavine, aminoacridine hydrochloride and methylene blue. ECHA hazards were found for each of these 4 components with worse case scenario reported here. There were two ethacridine lactate reported on ECHA so hazard statements from both were used. Aminoacridine hydrochloride is believed to refer to Acridin-9-ylamine hydrochloride monohydrate.
Non-toxic method. No ECHA entry or SDS sheet.
